Title: IWS - Verify component properties persist after save and reopen
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. Rename the component to "Lobby Sensor".
5. Modify additional properties (e.g., temperature unit to °F).
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save.

Title: 2. Close the design.
3. Reopen the saved design.
4. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.
5. Open Right Sidebar > Properties drawer.
2. All modified property values are retained exactly as saved.

IWS - Verify LED configuration persists after save and reopen
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is on the network, inventory item name matches device name in Configurator.
5. Configure the LED ring to a specific color (e.g., blue) and brightness (e.g., 75%).
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save.

Title: 2. Close the design.
3. Reopen the saved design.
4. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".
5. Select the "LED Configuration" tab and review the LED configuration values.
Preconditions: 1. The LED color is still set to blue.

Title: 2. The LED brightness is still set to 75%.
3. The physical device LED ring matches the saved configuration after design is redeployed.
IWS - Verify control pin wiring persists after save and reopen
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component and a Script component to the canvas.
4. Wire at least three output pins from IWS to the Script component.
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save.

Title: 2. Close the design.
3. Reopen the saved design.
4. Review the wiring on Center Workspace > Canvas.
Preconditions: 1. All wire connections between the IWS and Script components are intact.

Title: 2. The correct pins are connected as originally configured.
3. No broken or missing connections.
IWS - Verify device name assignment persists after save and reopen
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. Set the inventory item name to match the device name in Configurator.
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save.

Title: 2. Close the design.
3. Reopen the saved design.
4. Select the IWS component and review the inventory item name in Right Sidebar > Properties drawer.
5. Deploy the design to the Core.
Preconditions: 1. The inventory item name is retained exactly as saved.

Title: 2. On deploy, the device auto-connects (device name matches inventory item name).
3. No manual reassignment is required.
IWS - Verify design with IWS component can be saved as a new file
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas with full configuration (name, LED settings, zones, wiring).
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save As.

Title: 2. Choose a different file name and location.
3. Close the design.
4. Open the newly saved file.
5. Review all IWS component configuration.
2. All IWS component configuration is preserved in the new file.
3. The original file remains unchanged.
IWS - Verify multiple IWS components persist independently
2. Create a new design.
3. Add three IWS components to the canvas.
4. Configure each with unique names and LED settings.
Preconditions: 1. Save the design via Menu Bar > File > Save.

Title: 2. Close the design.
3. Reopen the saved design.
4. Review each IWS component's configuration individually.
Preconditions: 1. Each of the three IWS components retains its unique configuration.

Title: 2. Component names and LED settings are independent and correct.
3. No cross-contamination of settings between components.
